text.title: Message Queue Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying message-queues. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Consumer crashes mid-process; queue loses the message; downstream system never receives the event.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used auto-ack default 'because it was simpler'.
antipattern.fix: Switch to manual ack. Acknowledge only after the side-effect commit. Test with a kill-9 during processing to confirm redelivery.

antipattern.symptom: Payment processor double-charges customer because broker retried and consumer didn't have an idempotency guard.
antipattern.fix: Add idempotency key to message schema. Persist (key, processed_at) in a DB table. Reject retries that match a processed key.

antipattern.symptom: DLQ has accumulated 14k messages; nobody noticed because no alert was wired.
antipattern.fix: Wire alert_endpoint to DLQ depth metric. Threshold: depth > 10 OR rate > 1/minute. PagerDuty for severity-1; Slack for warning.

antipattern.symptom: Bad message retried 1000 times per second, pinning the worker pool and saturating the broker.
antipattern.fix: Declare max_retries (3-5) with exponential backoff + jitter. Move to DLQ on exhaustion. Add poison-message detector that drops on n consecutive same-payload failures.
